description: Add a new attribute to a metadata object. Supports: Catalog, Document, ExchangePlan, ChartOfCharacteristicTypes, ChartOfAccounts, ChartOfCalculationTypes, BusinessProcess, Task, DataProcessor, Report, InformationRegister, AccumulationRegister, AccountingRegister. The attribute is created with default properties. Russian type names are also supported.

expectedNotExists.description: Stale-intent guard (default: false). Set true to assert that, per the state you last read, the parent has NO attribute with this name. If one already exists, the add is rejected with a precondition error steering you to re-read — instead of the generic duplicate error. A duplicate attribute is rejected regardless of this flag; it only sharpens the diagnostic for a stale snapshot.

language.description: Language code for the synonym (e.g. 'ru', 'en'). If not specified, uses the configuration default language.

parentFqn.description: FQN of the parent object (e.g. 'Catalog.Products', 'Document.SalesOrder'). The metadata TYPE token may be English or Russian; the object name is the programmatic Name (not the synonym / display name).

synonym.description: Optional synonym (display name) for the attribute. Set for the configuration default language unless 'language' is specified.

name: add_metadata_attribute

description: Clean EDT project and trigger full revalidation. Refreshes files from disk, clears all validation markers, and waits for EDT to complete revalidation.

projectName.description: Name of the project to clean (optional, cleans all EDT projects if not specified)

name: clean_project

description: Create a new top-level metadata object with EDT default content. Supported types: Catalog, Document, InformationRegister, AccumulationRegister, Enum, CommonModule, Report, DataProcessor. The object is created with a properly generated UUID and default properties (same as the EDT 'New' wizard). Optionally sets synonym and comment. Russian type names are also supported.

comment.description: Optional comment for the new object.

expectedNotExists.description: Stale-intent guard (default: false). Set true to assert that, per the state you last read, this object does NOT yet exist. If it actually does, the create is rejected with a precondition error steering you to re-read — instead of the generic duplicate error. A creation always rejects a real duplicate regardless of this flag; it only sharpens the diagnostic for a stale snapshot.

language.description: Language code for the synonym (e.g. 'ru', 'en'). If not specified, uses the configuration default language.

metadataType.description: Metadata type to create (required): 'Catalog', 'Document', 'InformationRegister', 'AccumulationRegister', 'Enum', 'CommonModule', 'Report', 'DataProcessor'. Russian type names are also supported.

name.description: Name for the new object (required). Must be a valid 1C identifier.

synonym.description: Optional synonym (display name). Set for the configuration default language unless 'language' is specified.

name: create_metadata_object

description: Start an EDT debug session. Pass launchConfigurationName to run any existing EDT debug configuration by name (runtime client OR 'Attach to 1C:Enterprise Debug Server' — required for debugging server-side code: HTTP services, background jobs, scheduled jobs). Otherwise pass projectName + applicationId to launch the matching runtime-client config. If a previous launch of the same configuration is still alive, the tool short-circuits with `alreadyRunning: true` and does NOT spawn a fresh client — to force a clean restart (e.g. after code changes that require a new session), call `terminate_launch` first, then `debug_launch` again.

applicationId.description: Application ID from get_applications (required for runtime-client launches)

launchConfigurationName.description: Exact name of an EDT debug launch configuration (runtime client or Attach). Use for Attach configurations or to pick a specific client config by name.

projectName.description: EDT project name (required unless launchConfigurationName is given)

updateBeforeLaunch.description: If true — update database before launching (default: true, ignored for Attach)

name: debug_launch

description: Report active debug launches: applicationId (real or synthetic 'attach:<name>'), launch configuration name/type, mode (debug/run), whether the target is currently suspended, thread count, and the line of the top suspended frame. Optionally filter by applicationId.
name: debug_status

description: Launch YAXUnit tests in DEBUG mode so breakpoints fire. Returns immediately after the launch is queued — call wait_for_break next to block until a breakpoint is hit, then inspect with get_variables / evaluate_expression / step / resume. Use a tight tests filter (single test method) to make the cycle predictable. Requires an existing 1C launch configuration and YAXUnit installed in the infobase.

applicationId.description: Application id from get_applications (required if launchConfigurationName is omitted)

extensions.description: Comma-separated extension names to filter tests by extension

launchConfigurationName.description: Exact EDT runtime-client launch configuration name (preferred; from list_configurations)

modules.description: Comma-separated module names to filter tests

projectName.description: EDT project name (required if launchConfigurationName is omitted)

tests.description: Comma-separated test names in Module.Method format (recommended: pin to one test)

updateBeforeLaunch.description: Auto-chain (default: true): before spawning the debug launch, politely terminate any live 1С client running this configuration and run a silent DB update — so EDT's launch delegate does not pop its modal 'Update database?' dialog that would block the MCP call. Set false to keep legacy behaviour (delegate decides; dialog may appear).

name: debug_yaxunit_tests

description: Delete a metadata object or attribute with full refactoring support. Cleans up all references in BSL code, forms, and other metadata. First call without confirm to preview affected locations, then call with confirm=true to apply. Supports FQNs like 'Catalog.Products', 'Document.SalesOrder.Attribute.Amount'. Russian type names are also supported.

confirm.description: Set to true to execute the deletion. Default false = preview only.

objectFqn.description: FQN of the object to delete (e.g. 'Catalog.Products', 'Document.SalesOrder.Attribute.Amount'). The metadata TYPE token may be English or Russian; the object name is the programmatic Name (not the synonym / display name).

name: delete_metadata_object

description: Evaluate a BSL expression in the context of a suspended stack frame. Pass frameRef from wait_for_break and the expression text. WARNING: this executes arbitrary BSL code in the running 1C application.
name: evaluate_expression

description: Export an EDT configuration project to XML files (EDT menu: Export -> Configuration to XML Files). Equivalent of 1C platform DumpConfigToFiles.

outputPath.description: Filesystem path of the output directory for the XML files (required)

name: export_configuration_to_xml

description: Find all references to a metadata object. Returns all places where the object is used: in other metadata objects, in BSL code modules with line numbers, forms, roles, subsystems, etc. Supports both English and Russian metadata type names (e.g., 'Справочник.Номенклатура', 'Документ.Заказ').

limit.description: Result-size hint (default 100, max 500). Caps the overall number of references collected before grouping at limit*10, not per category.

objectFqn.description: Fully qualified name of the object to find references for (e.g. 'Catalog.Products', 'Document.SalesOrder', 'CommonModule.Common'). Russian type names are also supported (e.g. 'Справочник.Номенклатура')

name: find_references

description: Generate translation strings (.lstr/.trans/.dict) for a configuration project — scans translatable features and writes the resulting keys into the storages declared on the project. Equivalent of the EDT menu Translation -> Generate translation strings. Invoke on the configuration project (V8ConfigurationNature), NOT on a dictionary storage project (plain Eclipse project where .lstr/.trans/.dict live). Requires LanguageTool installed in EDT.

collectModelType.description: Model collection mode: ANY | NONE | COMPUTED_ONLY | UNKNOWN_ONLY | TAGS_ONLY. Default: ANY.

fillUpType.description: Pre-fill new keys with values from: NOT_FILLUP | FROM_SOURCE_LANGUAGE | FROM_PROVIDER. Default: NOT_FILLUP.

projectName.description: Configuration project name (V8ConfigurationNature). NOT a dictionary storage project (the plain Eclipse project where .lstr/.trans/.dict live) — pass the configuration whose translatable features should be scanned. Required.

providerId.description: Translation provider ID, used only when fillUpType=FROM_PROVIDER (e.g. "com.e1c.langtool.history.externalTranslationProvider"). Use get_translation_project_info to list available providers.

storageId.description: Storage ID to write generated keys into. Use get_translation_project_info to list available storages. Default: "edit:default".

targetLanguages.description: Target language codes to generate strings for, e.g. ["en"] (required).

name: generate_translation_strings

description: Get list of applications (infobases) for a project. Returns application ID, name, type, and update state. Application ID is required for update_database and debug_launch tools.
name: get_applications

description: Get bookmarks from the workspace. Returns bookmark message, file path, and line number.
name: get_bookmarks

description: Get detailed description of an EDT check by its ID. Returns markdown content with check explanation, examples, and how to fix. Requires a configured check-descriptions folder (MCP preferences); without it the tool returns a configuration error.
name: get_check_description

description: Get 1C:Enterprise configuration properties (name, synonym, comment, script variant, compatibility mode, etc.)

projectName.description: Project name (optional, if not specified returns first configuration project)

name: get_configuration_properties

description: Get content assist (code completion) proposals at a specific position in a BSL file. Opens the file in EDT editor and retrieves available completions at the given line and column.

contains.description: Filter proposals by display string containing these substrings (comma-separated, e.g. 'Insert,Add')

extendedDocumentation.description: Return full documentation (default: false, only display string)

limit.description: Maximum number of proposals to return (default: from preferences)

modulePath.description: BSL module path from project's src folder (e.g. 'CommonModules/MyModule/Module.bsl') (canonical; alias: filePath)

offset.description: Skip first N proposals (default: 0, for pagination)

name: get_content_assist

description: Returns the running 1C:EDT version as a plain version string. Returns "Unknown" when the version cannot be determined.
name: get_edt_version

description: Return a YAML snapshot of the active form WYSIWYG layout: calculated bounds, element types, and display-affecting properties. Can open and activate a form automatically. Requires EDT launched with -DnativeFormBufferedLayoutRender=true; a blank/empty result usually means that JVM flag is missing, not a bad call.

formPath.description: Metadata FQN path to the form. Format: 'MetadataType.ObjectName.Forms.FormName' or 'CommonForm.FormName'. Examples: 'Catalog.Products.Forms.ItemForm', 'Document.SalesOrder.Forms.DocumentForm', 'CommonForm.MyForm'. If not specified, uses the currently active form editor.

mode.description: Output mode: 'compact' (default) or 'full'. Compact returns only visual elements with positive bounds and selected display properties. Full returns all layout nodes and all non-containment properties.

projectName.description: EDT project name. Required when formPath is specified.

refresh.description: Force WYSIWYG refresh before snapshot (default: true)

name: get_form_layout_snapshot

description: Capture a screenshot of the active form WYSIWYG editor as PNG. Can open and activate a form automatically by metadata FQN path. Requires EDT launched with -DnativeFormBufferedLayoutRender=true; a blank/empty result usually means that JVM flag is missing, not a bad call.

formPath.description: Metadata FQN path to the form. Format: 'MetadataType.ObjectName.Forms.FormName' or 'CommonForm.FormName'. Examples: 'Catalog.Products.Forms.ItemForm', 'Document.SalesOrder.Forms.DocumentForm', 'CommonForm.MyForm'. If not specified, captures the currently active form editor.

projectName.description: EDT project name. Required when formPath is specified.

refresh.description: Force WYSIWYG refresh before capture (default: false)

name: get_form_screenshot

description: Read the editable model tree of a managed form: the nested items (groups, fields, tables) with their programmatic name, integer id and item type; the form attributes (name + value type); and the form commands (name + title). This is the form's editable model (the source of the item/attribute ids that form-write tools address), NOT the rendered WYSIWYG layout (use get_form_layout_snapshot for bounds). Reads the BM model directly; does not open the form editor.

formPath.description: Metadata FQN path to the form. Format: 'MetadataType.ObjectName.Forms.FormName' or 'CommonForm.FormName'. Examples: 'Catalog.Products.Forms.ItemForm', 'Document.SalesOrder.Forms.DocumentForm', 'CommonForm.MyForm'. The metadata TYPE token may be English or Russian; the object and form names are the programmatic Name (not the synonym / display name).

language.description: Language code for titles (e.g. 'en', 'ru'). Uses the configuration default if not specified.

projectName.description: EDT project name. Required when formPath is specified.

name: get_form_structure

description: Get detailed properties of metadata objects from 1C configuration. Returns basic info by default, or full details with 'full: true'. In full mode each reflection section is capped (a '[truncated]' row marks omitted rows); request fewer FQNs to keep the response small.

full.description: Return all properties (true) or only key info (false). Default: false

language.description: Language code for synonyms (e.g. 'en', 'ru'). Uses configuration default if not specified.

objectFqns.description: Array of FQNs (e.g. ['Catalog.Products', 'Document.SalesOrder']). Russian type names are also supported (e.g. 'Справочник.Номенклатура'). Required.

name: get_metadata_details

description: Get list of metadata objects from 1C configuration. Returns Name, Synonym, Comment, Type, ObjectModule, ManagerModule for each object. Supports filtering by metadata type.

language.description: Language code for synonyms (e.g. 'en', 'ru'). If not specified, uses configuration default language.

name: get_metadata_objects

description: Find method call hierarchy: who calls this method (callers) or what this method calls (callees). Uses semantic BSL AST analysis (resolved feature references).

direction.description: Direction: 'callers' (who calls this method, default) or 'callees' (what this method calls)

limit.description: Maximum number of results. Default: 100, max: 500

name: get_method_call_hierarchy

description: Get structure of a BSL module: all procedures/functions with signatures, line numbers, regions, execution context (&AtServer, &AtClient), export flag, and parameters.

includeComments.description: Include documentation comments for methods. Default: false

includeVariables.description: Include module-level variable declarations. Default: false

name: get_module_structure

description: Get metadata objects filtered by tags. Returns objects that have any of the specified tags, including tag descriptions and object FQNs.

limit.description: Maximum number of objects to return per tag. Default: 100

tags.description: Array of tag names to filter by (e.g. ['Important', 'NeedsReview']). Returns objects that have ANY of these tags. Required.

name: get_objects_by_tags

description: Get platform documentation for 1C:Enterprise types, methods, properties, and built-in functions. Examples: typeName='ValueTable', typeName='Array' memberName='Add'

category.description: Category: 'type' (platform types like ValueTable), 'builtin' (built-in functions). Default: 'type'

limit.description: Maximum number of results to return. Default: 50

memberName.description: Filter by member name (method/property). Supports partial match. Example: 'Add', 'Insert', 'Count'

projectName.description: EDT project name to determine platform version. Optional.

typeName.description: Type or symbol name (e.g. 'ValueTable', 'Array', 'Structure'). Supports both English and Russian names.

name: get_platform_documentation

description: Get problem summary with counts grouped by project and EDT severity level (ERRORS, BLOCKER, CRITICAL, MAJOR, MINOR, TRIVIAL).

projectName.description: Name of the project (optional, all projects if not specified)

name: get_problem_summary

description: Get profiling (performance measurement) results after a debug session. Returns per-module, per-line data: call count, timing, percentage. Also reports whether profiling is currently active (pass applicationId to check a specific session). Optionally filter by module name. Call after start_profiling + test run (and stop_profiling to finalize collection).

applicationId.description: Optional debug session id. When set, the response reports whether profiling is currently active (on/off) for that session.

minFrequency.description: Only include lines called at least N times (default: 1)

moduleFilter.description: Optional substring filter on module name

name: get_profiling_results

description: Get detailed configuration problems from EDT. Returns check code, description, object location, severity level (ERRORS, BLOCKER, CRITICAL, MAJOR, MINOR, TRIVIAL, NONE). For problems that point at a BSL module, also returns a structural locator (Module path + Line) you can feed straight into read_module_source or set_breakpoint. Can filter by specific objects using FQN (e.g. 'Document.SalesOrder', 'Catalog.Products'). Russian type names are also supported (e.g. 'Документ.ПриходнаяНакладная', 'Справочник.Номенклатура').

checkId.description: Filter by check ID substring. Matches either the symbolic check id (e.g. 'ql-temp-table-index') or the short UID (e.g. 'SU23') (optional)

objects.description: Filter by object FQNs (e.g. ['Document.SalesOrder', 'Catalog.Products']). Russian type names supported (e.g. 'Документ.ПродажаТоваров'). Returns errors only from these objects.

name: get_project_errors

description: Self-diagnosis snapshot of the running MCP server: listening port, MCP protocol version, plugin version, EDT version, enabled/total tool counts, the plainTextMode and checksFolderConfigured preference flags, the two form-render JVM flags (nativeFormBufferedLayoutRender / nativeFormLayoutRender), and whether authentication is enabled. Use it to explain a blank form screenshot or a plain-text JSON response. Never returns the auth token value or the checks folder path, only booleans.
name: get_server_status

description: Get content of a specific 1C subsystem: basic properties, list of metadata objects included in the subsystem (Type/Name/Synonym/FQN), and nested child subsystems. Subsystem is identified by FQN (e.g. 'Subsystem.Sales' or 'Subsystem.Sales.Subsystem.Orders'). Returns content of the requested subsystem only by default (recursive=false) to keep the response compact; set recursive=true to also include objects from nested subsystems (deduplicated).

language.description: Language code for synonyms (e.g. 'en', 'ru'). Uses configuration default if not specified.

recursive.description: Include objects from nested subsystems in the Content section (default: false)

name: get_subsystem_content

description: Get type/hover info about a symbol at a position in a BSL module. Returns inferred types, signatures, and documentation.

modulePath.description: BSL module path from src/, e.g. 'CommonModules/MyModule/Module.bsl' (canonical; alias: filePath)

name: get_symbol_info

description: Get list of all tags defined in the project. Tags are user-defined labels for organizing metadata objects. Returns tag name, color, description, and number of assigned objects.
name: get_tags

description: Get tasks (TODO, FIXME, etc.) from the workspace. Returns task message, file path, line number, and priority.
name: get_tasks

description: Get the full on-demand how-to for a tool: its description, every parameter (type, required, allowed values) and extended examples/preconditions kept OUT of the always-loaded tool list to save context. Pass the exact tool name from tools/list. Also available as the resource guide://<toolName>.

toolName.description: The exact name of the tool to document, as it appears in tools/list (e.g. 'read_module_source').

name: get_tool_guide

description: Return LanguageTool metadata for a project: translation storages declared on the project and available translation provider IDs. Requires EDT with LanguageTool installed. If the storages list is empty, the configuration has no dictionary storage attached yet. The user has to set this up in EDT manually (no MCP tool for it): create a plain Eclipse project (File -> New -> Project -> General -> Project), then attach it to the configuration via the configuration project's properties (Translation settings). Do NOT use any '1C:Enterprise -> Dependent translation project' wizard — the dictionary storage project must be a plain Eclipse project, and the configuration itself can also act as its own storage if no separate Eclipse project is desired.
name: get_translation_project_info

description: Read variables from a stack frame of a suspended debug thread. Pass frameRef from wait_for_break (preferred) or threadId+frameIndex. Use expandPath to drill into nested structures (dot-separated).

expandPath.description: Dot-separated path to expand a nested variable

frameRef.description: Stable frame reference returned from wait_for_break

name: get_variables

description: Go to definition of a symbol — the inverse of find_references. Accepts three forms: 'ModuleName.MethodName' (qualified method), 'MethodName' (unqualified — also pass modulePath), and a metadata FQN like 'Catalog.Products'. Supports Russian type names.

modulePath.description: Module path from src/. Required for unqualified method names.

symbol.description: Formats: 'ModuleName.MethodName', 'MethodName' (needs modulePath), 'Catalog.Products' (metadata FQN). Russian type names supported.

name: go_to_definition

description: Import a configuration from a directory of XML files into a new EDT project (EDT menu: Import). The reverse of export_configuration_to_xml.

importPath.description: Filesystem path of the source directory containing XML files (required)

projectName.description: Name of the new EDT project to create in the workspace (required)

projectNature.description: EDT project nature ID, e.g. 'com._1c.g5.v8.dt.core.V8ConfigurationNature'. Pass empty string to let EDT auto-detect.

xmlVersion.description: XML format version, e.g. '8.3.20'. Pass empty string to let EDT auto-detect.

name: import_configuration_from_xml

description: List active line breakpoints. Optionally filter by projectName.
name: list_breakpoints

description: List EDT launch configurations (runtime client + Attach + other 1C types) with their current running state. Each entry carries the configuration name (use it as launchConfigurationName in debug_launch / run_yaxunit_tests / debug_yaxunit_tests / update_database), type id, attach flag, applicationId (real or synthetic 'attach:<name>'), project, infobase alias, debug server URL, and a 'running' flag (plus 'suspended' when the session is paused on a breakpoint). Use type='attach' for server-side debug setups (HTTP services, background jobs), type='client' for 1C:Enterprise client configs, or type='all' (default).

type.description: Filter: 'attach' (RemoteRuntime + LocalRuntime), 'client' (RuntimeClient), or 'all' (default — any 1C/EDT launch config)

name: list_configurations

description: List all BSL modules in an EDT project. Can filter by metadata type (documents, catalogs, commonModules, etc.) or by specific object name. Returns module path, type, and parent object.

objectName.description: Name of specific metadata object to list modules for (e.g. 'Products')

name: list_modules

description: List all workspace projects with properties (name, path, type, natures)
name: list_projects

description: List 1C subsystems of a configuration. Returns a flat table with FQN (e.g. 'Subsystem.Sales' or 'Subsystem.Sales.Subsystem.Orders'), Synonym, Comment, IncludeInCommandInterface flag, content count (objects in subsystem) and children count (nested subsystems). Walks the whole tree by default (recursive=true) so AI sees all subsystems at once; set recursive=false to list only top-level subsystems. Use 'get_subsystem_content' to inspect a specific subsystem and its objects.

language.description: Language code for synonyms (e.g. 'en', 'ru'). Uses configuration default if not specified.

limit.description: Maximum number of results. Default: from preferences (100)

nameFilter.description: Partial name match filter (case-insensitive, matches Name only — not Synonym)

name: list_subsystems

description: Read a specific procedure/function from a BSL module by name. Returns source code with metadata. Lists available methods if not found.
name: read_method_source

description: Read BSL module source code from EDT project. Returns YAML frontmatter (projectName, module, contentHash, startLine, endLine, totalLines; plus truncated: true, nextStartLine and hint when the range was clamped by the configured line limit) followed by clean source in a fenced bsl block (no line-number prefixes). contentHash is an opaque whole-file revision token: pass it back as write_module_source's expectedHash to guard against a lost update. For an empty file, startLine/endLine are omitted and totalLines is 0. Supports reading full file or a specific line range.

endLine.description: End line number (1-based, inclusive). If omitted, reads to end.

modulePath.description: Path from src/ folder, e.g. 'CommonModules/MyModule/Module.bsl' or 'Documents/SalesOrder/ObjectModule.bsl' (required)

startLine.description: Start line number (1-based, inclusive). If omitted, reads from beginning.

name: read_module_source

description: Remove a 1C BSL line breakpoint. Either pass breakpointId (returned from set_breakpoint) or projectName+module+lineNumber to look it up by coordinates.

lineNumber.description: 1-based line number (when looking up by coordinates)

modulePath.description: EDT module path or absolute path (when looking up by coordinates)

projectName.description: EDT project name (when looking up by coordinates)

name: remove_breakpoint

description: Rename a metadata object or attribute with full refactoring support. Updates all references in BSL code, forms, and other metadata. WORKFLOW: 1) Call without confirm to get a preview with all change points and their indices. 2) Review the preview — each change has an index, file, description, and enabled state. 3) Call with confirm=true. Optionally pass disableIndices to skip specific change points. Supports FQNs like 'Catalog.Products', 'Document.SalesOrder.Attribute.Amount'. Russian type names are also supported.

confirm.description: Set to true to execute the rename. Default false = preview only.

disableIndices.description: Comma-separated indices of change points to SKIP (from the preview list). Only optional changes can be disabled. Example: '2,3,5'

maxResults.description: Max number of change points to show in preview (default 20). 0 = no limit.

objectFqn.description: FQN of the object to rename (e.g. 'Catalog.Products', 'Document.SalesOrder.Attribute.Amount'). Russian names supported.

name: rename_metadata_object

description: Resume a suspended debug thread or all threads of a debug target. Pass threadId (from wait_for_break) or applicationId. With no arguments, resumes the single active debug launch if exactly one exists.

applicationId.description: Application id (real or 'attach:<configName>' — resumes all threads of this target)

name: resume

description: Revalidate EDT project or specific objects. If objects array is empty or missing, revalidates entire project. FQN examples: 'Document.SalesOrder', 'Catalog.Products', 'CommonModule.Common'. Russian type names are also supported (e.g. 'Документ.ПриходнаяНакладная', 'Справочник.Номенклатура').

objects.description: FQNs to revalidate (e.g. ['Document.SalesOrder']). Russian type names supported (e.g. 'Документ.ПродажаТоваров'). Empty array = full project revalidation

name: revalidate_objects

description: Run YAXUnit tests for a 1C:Enterprise project. Launches the application with RunUnitTests parameter, polls for completion for up to `timeout` seconds (default 60), then returns the JUnit Markdown report. If the launch is still running when the polling window expires, returns **Pending** — call this tool again with the same arguments to keep waiting and fetch the result once the launch finishes. The launch is NOT terminated on timeout. A full Markdown report is also written to report.md next to junit.xml. Requires an existing launch configuration and YAXUnit extension installed in the infobase.

applicationId.description: Application ID from get_applications (required if launchConfigurationName is omitted)

extensions.description: Comma-separated extension names to filter tests by extension

launchConfigurationName.description: Exact EDT runtime-client launch configuration name (preferred; from list_configurations)

modules.description: Comma-separated module names to filter tests

projectName.description: EDT project name (required if launchConfigurationName is omitted)

tests.description: Comma-separated test names in Module.Method format

timeout.description: Polling window in seconds (default: 60). On expiry returns Pending; call again to keep waiting.

updateBeforeLaunch.description: Auto-chain (default: true): before spawning a new test launch, politely terminate any live 1С client running this configuration and run a silent DB update — so EDT's launch delegate does not pop its modal 'Update database?' dialog that would block the MCP call. Set false to keep legacy behaviour (delegate decides; dialog may appear).

name: run_yaxunit_tests

description: Literal/regex full-text search across all BSL modules in a project. Supports plain text and regex patterns, case sensitivity, context lines around matches, and file path filtering. NOT dialect-aware: matching is purely textual, so a query spelled in one BSL language (e.g. the English 'EndProcedure') will NOT find the Russian-spelled equivalent and vice versa. To find an identifier regardless of ru/en spelling, use get_symbol_info, find_references or get_method_call_hierarchy instead. Use outputMode 'count' or 'files' for lightweight queries before full search.

contextLines.description: Lines of context before/after each match. Default: 2, max: 5

fileMask.description: Filter by module path substring (e.g. 'CommonModules' or 'Documents/SalesOrder')

isRegex.description: Treat query as regular expression. Default: false

limit.description: Maximum number of matches to return with context. Default: 100, max: 500

maxResults.description: Deprecated alias for 'limit' (Maximum number of matches). Default: 100, max: 500

metadataType.description: Filter by metadata type: 'documents', 'catalogs', 'commonModules', 'informationRegisters', 'accumulationRegisters', 'reports', 'dataProcessors', 'exchangePlans', 'businessProcesses', 'tasks', 'constants', 'commonCommands', 'commonForms', 'webServices', 'httpServices'. More precise than fileMask.

outputMode.description: Output mode: 'full' (matches with context, default), 'count' (only total count, fast), 'files' (file list with match counts, no context)

query.description: Search string or regex pattern (required). Matched literally/as regex against module text; NOT ru/en dialect-aware, so one language spelling will not match the other. For identifier lookup use get_symbol_info / find_references / get_method_call_hierarchy.

name: search_in_code

description: Set a line breakpoint on a 1C BSL module. Accepts either an EDT module-relative path (e.g. 'CommonModules/Foo/Module.bsl') or an absolute filesystem path. Use wait_for_break afterwards to block until the breakpoint is hit.

modulePath.description: Module identifier — EDT module path (CommonModules/Foo/Module.bsl) or absolute file path (required)

projectName.description: EDT project name (required when modulePath is module-relative)

name: set_breakpoint

description: Start performance measurement (замер производительности) on the active debug target. Enables line-level profiling: call counts and timing for every executed BSL line. Start-only and idempotent: if profiling is already active for this applicationId it stays on. Call stop_profiling to stop, then get_profiling_results to see which code was covered. Requires an active debug session (debug_launch or debug_yaxunit_tests).

applicationId.description: Application id of the running debug session (required)

name: start_profiling

description: Step a suspended debug thread. kind ∈ {over, into, out}. Blocks until the next SUSPEND event (or timeout) and returns the new frame snapshot.
name: step

description: Stop performance measurement (замер производительности) on the active debug target. Counterpart to start_profiling: deterministically switches profiling off. Idempotent: if profiling is not active for this applicationId it returns a benign result, not an error. Call get_profiling_results afterwards to retrieve the collected coverage.

applicationId.description: Application id of the running debug session (required)

name: stop_profiling

description: Terminate one or more 1С launches that were started from THIS EDT instance (runtime-client or Attach). Only launches visible via the Eclipse launch manager can be affected — 1С clients started externally (Designer, ad-hoc 1cv8c.exe, another EDT) are never touched. Select target via launchConfigurationName, or projectName+applicationId, or all=true (requires confirm=true). Attach configurations are disconnected (the 1С server keeps running) — set includeAttach=false to skip them. Use list_configurations first to see what is currently running.

all.description: Terminate every live EDT launch (optionally narrowed by projectName). Requires confirm=true. Default: false.

applicationId.description: Application ID from get_applications. Requires projectName.

confirm.description: Must be true when all=true. Guard against accidental mass termination.

force.description: If a polite ILaunch.terminate() does not finish within timeoutSeconds, escalate to IProcess.terminate() (OS-level kill). May lose unsaved 1С state. Default: false. Ignored for Attach.

includeAttach.description: Whether to act on Attach configurations (RemoteRuntime/LocalRuntime). When true, Attach launches are disconnected (the 1С cluster keeps running). Default: true.

launchConfigurationName.description: Exact EDT launch configuration name (from list_configurations) to terminate.

projectName.description: EDT project name. Combine with applicationId for a single launch, or with all=true to narrow 'terminate all' to one project.

timeoutSeconds.description: How long to wait for a polite termination per launch. Default is configured in EDT preferences (MCP Server → Tools → terminate_launch), factory default 10. Clamped to [1, 120].

name: terminate_launch

description: Run EDT 'Translate configuration' on a configuration project - reads the dictionaries from the storages bound to it (external dictionary storage projects with the dependentProjectNature, or in-configuration storages) and regenerates the translated artifacts. Equivalent of the context-menu action Translation -> Translate configuration. Requires LanguageTool installed in EDT.

projectName.description: Project name (typically the source, e.g. the ru project). Required.

targetLanguages.description: Target language codes to synchronize (e.g. ["en"]). Required.

name: translate_configuration

description: Update database (infobase) for an application. Target the application either by launchConfigurationName (preferred; from list_configurations) or by projectName + applicationId (from get_applications). Supports full update (complete reload) and incremental update (changes only). IMPORTANT: if a 1С client launched from this EDT is currently running against the target infobase, the update typically fails because the IB is held in exclusive use. Check `list_configurations` for `running: true`; if so, call `terminate_launch` first (it only affects launches started from this EDT instance), then retry.

applicationId.description: Application ID from get_applications (required if launchConfigurationName is omitted)

autoRestructure.description: Automatically apply restructurization if needed (default: true)

fullUpdate.description: If true - full reload, if false - incremental update (default: false)

launchConfigurationName.description: Exact EDT runtime-client launch configuration name (preferred; from list_configurations)

projectName.description: EDT project name (required if launchConfigurationName is omitted)

name: update_database

description: Validate 1C:Enterprise query language (QL) text in the context of a project. Parses query text and returns syntax and semantic errors with line numbers. Supports both regular queries and DCS (Data Composition System) queries.

dcsMode.description: DCS (Data Composition System) mode. Set to true for queries used in data composition schemas. Allows additional DCS-specific syntax. Default: false

queryText.description: Query text to validate (required). The full text of the 1C query, e.g. 'SELECT Ref FROM Catalog.Products WHERE Description LIKE &SearchString'

name: validate_query

description: Wait for a debug suspend event (e.g. breakpoint hit) on the given application. Returns the suspended thread/frame snapshot, or {hit:false} on timeout. applicationId may be real or synthetic 'attach:<configName>'. If omitted and exactly one EDT debug launch is active, that launch is used. Does NOT terminate the launch on timeout — call again to keep waiting.

applicationId.description: Application id of the running debug session (real or 'attach:<configName>'). Optional if exactly one debug launch is active.

name: wait_for_break

description: Write BSL source code to 1C metadata object modules. Modes: searchReplace (find oldSource and replace with source, default), replace (replace entire file), append (add to end). Target the module EITHER by modulePath OR by objectName (these are mutually exclusive — pass exactly one, never both, never neither). Conditional parameters (each required only in its case): moduleType only with objectName (ignored/rejected with modulePath); oldSource only for mode=searchReplace (required there); formName only for moduleType=FormModule (required there, except CommonForm); commandName only for moduleType=CommandModule (required there, except CommonCommand). Optional expectedHash (the contentHash from your last read_module_source / read_method_source) rejects the write if the module changed since you read it — a cheap lost-update guard that works for any mode. Automatically checks BSL syntax (balanced Procedure/EndProcedure, Function/EndFunction, If/EndIf, etc.) before writing — blocks write on errors. Pass skipSyntaxCheck=true to force.

expectedHash.description: Lost-update guard for ANY mode over an EXISTING module. Pass the opaque contentHash you got from read_module_source / read_method_source; if it no longer matches the current file (someone else edited it), the write is rejected so you can re-read and retry. Cheaper than expectedSource (a fixed-size token, not the whole file). Ignored when creating a new module.

expectedSource.description: Lost-update guard for mode=replace over an EXISTING module. Pass the exact module content you last read with read_module_source; if it no longer matches the current file (someone else edited it), the write is rejected so you can re-read and retry. Ignored for searchReplace/append and when creating a new module.

mode.description: Write mode: 'searchReplace' (find oldSource and replace with source, default), 'replace' (replace entire file), 'append' (add to end).

modulePath.description: Path to module from src/ folder, e.g. 'Documents/MyDoc/ObjectModule.bsl' or 'CommonModules/MyModule/Module.bsl'. Alternative: use objectName + moduleType.

moduleType.description: Module type (used with objectName): ObjectModule (default), ManagerModule, FormModule, CommandModule, RecordSetModule, Module.

objectName.description: Full object name, e.g. 'Document.MyDoc', 'DataProcessor.MyProcessor'. Supports Russian names (e.g. 'Документ.МойДок'). Alternative to modulePath.

oldSource.description: Existing code to find and replace (required for searchReplace mode). Must match exactly one location in the file. Proves that you have read the current file content.

overwrite.description: Force mode=replace to overwrite an EXISTING module without an expectedSource lost-update check (default: false). Ignored for searchReplace/append and when creating a new module.

skipSyntaxCheck.description: Skip BSL syntax validation (default: false). By default, checks balanced Procedure/EndProcedure, Function/EndFunction, If/EndIf, While/EndDo, For/EndDo, Try/EndTry. Set true to force write.

source.description: BSL source code to write (required). For replace: complete module content. For searchReplace: new code replacing oldSource. For append: code to add.

name: write_module_source
